user_query:
I’m preparing a short policy brief on Ukraine’s public education spending for colleagues and the Ministry. Please build a small, reproducible prototype that downloads an official dataset, produces tidy outputs, and drafts a message I can send.

Data to download (Internet required):
- Source: World Bank Open Data API (official worldbank.org domain)
- Indicator: SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS (Government expenditure on education, total, % of GDP)
- Save the raw CSV exactly to: downloads/wb_SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS.csv

Local inputs to use:
- input/countries.csv (ISO3 filter list)
- input/email_context.yaml (recipient and context)

Processing requirements:
- Parse the downloaded CSV and filter to the countries listed in input/countries.csv.
- Keep years 2005–2022 inclusive. Drop rows where the indicator value is missing.
- Produce a tidy CSV at output/education_spending_tidy.csv with columns, in this order:
  1) country_iso3 (ISO3 code, must match input/countries.csv)
  2) country_name (as provided by the World Bank)
  3) year (YYYY)
  4) value_pct_gdp (numeric, dot as decimal separator)
  5) trailing_3yr_avg (numeric; for year t, average of t, t-1, t-2 when all three exist; otherwise leave blank)
- Create a summary JSON at output/summary.json with keys:
  - indicator_code ("SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS")
  - source ("World Bank Open Data")
  - countries_included (array of ISO3 codes actually present in output/education_spending_tidy.csv)
  - years_coverage: {"min_year": <int>, "max_year": <int>} taken from the tidy CSV across all included countries
  - records_count (integer number of rows in the tidy CSV)
  - download_timestamp (ISO 8601 string for when the CSV was saved)
  - checksum_sha256 (hex digest of downloads/wb_SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS.csv raw bytes)

Email draft (local file, no sending):
- Create a plain-text draft at output/email_draft.txt addressed to the recipient in input/email_context.yaml.
- The draft must begin with a subject line: "Subject: Preliminary education spending brief — [project_title]"
- Include greeting: "Dear [recipient_name],"
- In the body, include a short summary for Ukraine specifically, computed from output/education_spending_tidy.csv:
  - Coverage: earliest and latest year available for Ukraine in the tidy CSV within 2005–2022
  - Latest available year and value for Ukraine
  - Average over the last 5 available years for Ukraine (use the most recent 5 non-missing years in the tidy CSV)
- Clearly cite the data source as "World Bank Open Data, indicator SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS".
- Mention both attachments by path: output/education_spending_tidy.csv and output/summary.json.
- Request feedback by the 'deadline' date from input/email_context.yaml.
- End with a sign-off including sender_name from the YAML.

General constraints:
- Use only the official World Bank Open Data API (worldbank.org domain) for the download; do not hardcode values. Save the raw CSV and compute all statistics from it.
- Read both input files (countries.csv and email_context.yaml) and make them essential to the workflow (filtering and message content, respectively).
- Ensure all specified output files are created at the exact paths given and are parsable under the described structures.
- Do not require credentials or any private systems. All work should be reproducible in a normal local workspace.

Deliverables to produce in this workspace:
- downloads/wb_SE.XPD.TOTL.GD.ZS.csv (downloaded)
- output/education_spending_tidy.csv (tidy dataset)
- output/summary.json (metadata and checksum)
- output/email_draft.txt (message for the recipient) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
